items.title: Van Persie over absentie Ueda, Tengstedt en schorsingen Feyenoord
items.summary: Robin van Persie kon eindelijk weer eens juichen ná een wedstrijd. Feyenoord trok met hangen en wurgen de zege bij FC Utrecht over de streep. De trainer sprak over aanknopingspunten, het gemis van Ayase Ueda, de rol van Casper Tengstedt en de gele kaarten van Luciano Valente en Timon Wellenreuther.

items.title: Brighton v Crystal Palace: Premier League – live
items.summary: Kick-off at Amex Stadium 2pm (GMT) Email Daniel with your thoughts I’ll probably end up looking silly, but I quite fancy Palace here. Brighton lack a reliable scorer – though Katsoulas’ brilliant goal against Bournemouth tells us he knows where the goal is – and I think Palace have the speed of foot and of pass to cause them problems. So where is the game? Brighton will expect – and probably allowed – to have more of the ball, with Mitoma and Rutter staying narrow and Kadioglu and De Cuyper keeping width outside them – especially useful when facing a three-at-the-back system. The space will be in behind the wing-backs and down the sides of the centre-backs, though I’d also expect Katsoulas to target the space in behind. Continue reading...

items.title: Van Persie grapt over multi-inzetbare Moder: 'Volgende week keeper'
items.summary: Door de schorsing van Timon Wellenreuther moet Robin van Persie voor het thuisduel met Go Ahead Eagles een andere doelman aanwijzen. De trainer van Feyenoord wees met een knipoog naar Jakub Moder, die in het gewonnen uitduel met FC Utrecht als gelegenheidsspits speelde.

items.title: Arsenal 1-0 Manchester City: Women’s Super League – live reaction
items.summary: Olivia Smith’s strike ends the WSL leader’s wining run 3 min: Caitlin Foord turns Kerstin Casparij inside out on the left. She drives towards the byline before crossing the ball across the box. Nobody’s there to tap the ball in though. 1 min: Former Arsenal player Vivianne Miedema has an early opportunity. Manchester City win the ball off Arsenal shortly after kick off and Miedema strikes from about 25 yards out. It travels comfortably past the post. Continue reading...

items.title: VI Live: Telstar houdt stand in Deventer, Verheydt zet Willem II naast RKC
items.summary: Zondag staat ons een fraaie voetbaldag te wachten. De duels van Feyenoord, Ajax en PSV volgen we in aparte liveblogs, maar in deze VI Live blijf je op de hoogte van alle andere duels. Bijvoorbeeld van de Engelse topper tussen Liverpool en Manchester City.

items.title: Valente over keuze voor rugnummer 10: 'Kijk nog steeds heel veel video's van hem'
items.summary: Feyenoord heeft het uitduel met FC Utrecht zondag met klein verschil gewonnen . Hieronder lees je alles nog eens terug en vind je de laatste reacties.

items.title: Sheff Wed set unwanted record as Swansea run riot
items.summary: Sheffield Wednesday set an unwanted club record of nine league games without a goal as Swansea strolled to a 4-0 Sky Bet Championship win.

items.title: VI Live: AZ en Ajax maken er geen spektakelstuk van, grootste kans voor Bounida
items.summary: AZ neemt het zondagmiddag om 14.30 uur andermaal op tegen Ajax. De Alkmaarders stonden een kleine maand geleden ook al tegenover Ajax, dat zich wil revancheren voor de pijnlijke 6-0 nederlaag in de Eurojackpot KNVB Beker. Volg de Eredivisie-ontmoeting tussen AZ en Ajax in deze VI Live.

items.title: Liverpool signing Jacquet suffers 'serious' injury
items.summary: Liverpool 's new summer signing Jérémy Jacquet has sustained a "serious" shoulder injury, according to Rennes head coach Habib Beye.

items.title: Bucks' Antetokounmpo invests in Chelsea Women
items.summary: NBA star Giannis Antetokounmpo has announced he has joined the ownership group of Chelsea Women which includes his "friend" Reddit co-founder Alexis Ohanian.

items.title: Foxborough demands clarity on $8M WC funding
items.summary: Town officials in Foxborough, Massachusetts, are seeking clarity on the source of around $8 million they say is needed to fund public safety for this summer's World Cup games at Gillette Stadium, the home of the New England Patriots.

items.title: Wrexham hero Mullin hits out at club after exit
items.summary: Paul Mullin has hit out at parent club Wrexham, saying he's "never going to accept" the way he was frozen out of the team over the past 18 months.

items.title: England avoid huge shock at T20 World Cup after edging Nepal in thriller
items.summary: Two-time champions England dodged a huge shock at the T20 World Cup as they got their campaign under way with a nerve-jangling, four-run victory over a fearless Nepal in Mumbai.

items.title: Newcastle at lowest moment since Saudi takeover
items.summary: Saturday night’s post-match press conference at Newcastle felt like a big moment for Eddie Howe. It felt emotional – and he picked his words very carefully after the defeat to Brentford.

items.title: Curran holds nerve as England avoid Nepal upset - as it happened
Liverpool signing Jacquet injures shoulder
items.summary: Defender Jeremy Jacquet, who will join Liverpool at the end of the season, injures a shoulder in Rennes' defeat at Lens.

items.title: ‘We lived a miraculous thing’: Castel di Sangro, 30 years on from their epic rise
items.summary: Small town club’s Serie B adventure captivated football and inspired a famous book. That spirit remains and is being passed to their successors The WhatsApp group flickers into life at about 6am every day. It is the manager who goes first because, when you are 79, old habits die hard. “Good morning,” Osvaldo Jaconi hails his former players and staff before, little by little, the salutations roll in from across Italy. Maybe it is someone’s birthday or another special occasion; the conversation may be accelerated by an in-joke that recalls why, three decades ago, they were brought together in the first place. Just in case anyone could forget, the group’s title says: “Serie B.” This is how miracles stay alive. Perhaps it is the point of what Castel di Sangro achieved in 1995-96. A rag-tag bunch from this backwater in mountainous Abruzzo had risen from local amateur leagues and then, in a crowning triumph with little precedent, made it to the second tier. “It’s like 30 years haven’t passed,” says Angelo Petrarca, who was nominally the masseur but often resembled a one-man backroom. “It shows how much love everybody has for each other, and did back then. As if everybody is still right here.” Continue reading...

items.title: The new top-flight Canadian club inspired by Athletic Bilbao
items.summary: Supra du Quebec kick off their inaugural Canadian Premier League in April and will field only players born or raised in the province.

items.title: How Ekitike has already become a Liverpool cult hero
items.summary: Inside the rise of Hugo Ekitike, adapting to life in England, connecting with Florian Wirtz and why he's already a fan favourite.

items.title: How did Arsenal 'unlock' Sunderland's defence?
items.summary: Match of the Day pundit Alan Shearer explains how a "piece of brilliance" combined with effective substitutions allowed Arsenal to break down Sunderland's defence, in their 3-0 home win in the Premier League.
